originalTitle: Mexico v England: World Cup 2026 last 16 updates – live
summary: Minute-by-minute updates: Thomas Tuchel’s England side face a huge test against the co-hosts in Mexico City’s stormy Azteca Stadium. Join our writers
content: 12 min Pickford gets his hands on the ball for the first time in open play, as Alvarado’s pass bounces too far for his teammates. 10 min Long possession for England. Lira shields Bellingham away from the ball and is stunned that there’s no foul and further stunned when the throw-in goes to England. 9 min I am not correct. England run an overly complicated routine off the set piece. 8 min England pass around in their own half, not finding many options going forward. They find Bellingham, who plays left to Gordon, who draws a corner kick. I’m predicting a goal here. 6 min Lira fouls near midfield, then tries to pick up the ball and walk off with it. Kane comes over to try to retrieve it. The referee has a word with Kane but then calls over Lira, who is astounded and angry. 4 min Mexican fans are eager to chant ole with every pass. They had to be patient while one player considered options. An overeager Sánchez fouls Gordon, allowing England a free kick to relieve the pressure. 3 min Romo is still down. Bellingham and Kane had a chat with Lira in the small group of players standing around the injured player, which is rather strange. 1 min YELLOW to Declan Rice, who had a high boot while that caught the onrushing Romo, who stays down. The game is indeed underway. Anthems It’s difficult to imagine a crowd singing their national anthem with as much fervor as the Mexican crowd just did. England will likely need to weather an early storm here in more ways than one. Officials The referee is Alireza Faghani, who emigrated from Iran to Australia in 2019. He also refereed the France-Senegal game, drawing some criticism for sticking with a decision not to award a penalty kick after Sadio Mané upended Kylian Mbappé, choosing to stick with his initial decision after reviewing the replay at the VAR’s behest. His other game so far in this World Cup was the 0-0 draw between Colombia and Portugal. The ARs are also from Australia. Nicolas Gallo (Colombia) has the VAR assignment. The game is still scheduled to kick off in 15 minutes. Forecast? Very wet. But no lightning. Knock on whatever wood you can can find. More detail on the Balogun situation … And more mail … Carl Remmer: “Justin is thinking of the Mayans with their rudimentary basketball/football hybrid. The acoustics are so good in the arena they played in (even in the partly dilapidated state when I went there) that you can here a whisper from 50m away.” Jen Oram: “There’s some doubt about Aztec ball game sacrifices - some historians think the bouncing heads may actually have been those of the winning team, granted an early entry into heaven for their valour. (What happened to the losers, who knows.)” Postgame press conference with the losing team: “They wanted it more.” Will Christian: “England fans watching their national team at an ungodly hour? Aw. Shenanigans in Mexico? Sad. Just wait until the batteries and bags start flying. Enjoy your CONCACAF moment, England! PS - I described CONCACAF to a British friend as ‘Imagine you’re playing Scotland, in the sleet, on a grocery store parking lot in Aberdeen, with shopping trolleys still in it.’” Pam: “In response to Dave Hogg’s comment: I shudder to think of playing not mere 90 minutes, but the possibility of 120 minutes plus the penalty shootout drama in front of a partisan Azteca.” William Lee-Wright: “Naturalized Norwegian citizen here, moved from England 16 years ago, writing from Murcia in Spain, where we’re coming down from the electric experience of seeing Norway boss Brazil. My son Severin and I are staying up to see to see who Norway will play in the QF. We’re already discussing the merits of half and half face paint. I’m genuinely torn. Losing to Mexico tonight would be better than getting beaten by the row boys - my (football) life in Trondheim would be unbearable!” Richard Bowden (one of several prophecies): “In 1100, King Henry I, is associated with the early use of the lion standard. Fast forward exactly 926 years and you land in 2026. That number stands out straight away: 926 looks like 96 (the Queen’s age when she died) and 26 (the year of the final) combined into one cycle. Or you can add a 1, you get 1926, the year the Queen was born, the one person who handed England the World Cup trophy in 1966!” Matt Haythorne: “I’m in Vietnam and another hour rain delay will mean I can watch the second half as I’ll have finished work for the morning. I thought I could watch the first half, as it stands my 2 hours of work today (it’s a hard life) fall directly over the match time.” Kevin Dwyer: “I was at the Azteca as a young man of 21 back in 1970. Crap seats really high up at the back but what an atmosphere. Four years of saving on an apprentice wage but worth it to watch some football legends in the flesh. I am desperately trying to stay awake, no doubt I will nod off and wake up at the final whistle.” I’m hoping that doesn’t happen to me. Mary Waltz: “Beau, who has more experience playing on a rainy soggy pitch, Mexico or England?” Here’s something I don’t know – how often does England have torrential rain and/or thunderstorms? The stereotype is that it’s a 24/7 drizzle. The thunderstorms in the southern USA and Mexico, on the other hand, are intense. Julian Merz: “I’m watching in Sweden with a few stragglers from the Norway game who don’t have to get up early/at all tomorrow. The weather delay seems to have taken the wind out of their sails a bit though.” Daniel Whitehead: “Wigan fan here, watching in Idaho with my in-laws, writing to share a funny moment on the Fox commentary: they’re talking about the conditions, and Peter Crouch in a poncho is being asked by a commentator half his size to explain what he means by England’s experience of ‘Tuesday night games in Stoke’.” James Beesley with an addition to the All-Time Elevation XI: “Keith ‘Dizzy’ Gillespie?” I’m going to steer away of most email related to politics (the points have been made), but if I get something as clever as this, great …
chineseTitle: 墨西哥 vs 英格兰：2026 年世界杯最后 16 场更新 - 直播
chineseSummary: 每分钟更新：托马斯·图赫尔的英格兰队在墨西哥城风雨交加的阿兹台克体育场面临着与东道主的巨大考验。加入我们的作家
chineseContent: 第 12 分钟，皮克福德在空战中第一次拿到球，因为阿尔瓦拉多的传球弹得太远，对队友来说太远了。 10分钟，英格兰队长控球。里拉挡住了贝林厄姆，让他远离球，他惊讶地发现没有犯规，当英格兰队掷界外球时，他更加震惊了。 9分钟我不正确。英格兰队在定位球后的动作过于复杂。第8分钟，英格兰在本方半场传球，没有找到太多前进的选择。他们找到了贝林厄姆，贝林厄姆在左路传给戈登，戈登开出角球。我在这里预测一个进球。第6分钟，里拉在中场附近犯规，然后试图接球走开。凯恩过来试图取回它。裁判与凯恩说了几句话，但随后叫来了里拉，里拉感到震惊和愤怒。 4分钟墨西哥球迷在每次传球时都渴望高喊奥莱。当一名球员考虑选择时，他们必须保持耐心。过于急切的桑切斯对戈登犯规，让英格兰队获得任意球缓解压力。 3分钟后，罗莫仍然倒地。贝林汉姆和凯恩在受伤球员周围的一小群球员中与里拉聊天，这颇为奇怪。第 1 分钟，德克兰·赖斯 (Declan Rice) 吃到黄牌，他的靴子很高，抓住了冲上来的罗莫 (Romo)，但罗莫仍趴在地上。比赛确实正在进行中。国歌 很难想象会有一群人像墨西哥人那样热情地唱国歌。英格兰可能需要以多种方式来度过这场早期的风暴。裁判是阿里雷扎·法加尼 (Alireza Faghani)，他于 2019 年从伊朗移民到澳大利亚。他还执法了法国与塞内加尔的比赛，在萨迪奥·马内 (Sadio Mané) 推翻凯利安·姆巴佩 (Kylian Mbappé) 后，他因坚持不判点球的决定而受到一些批评，在审查了裁判后，他选择坚持最初的决定
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
